nodes.name: User
nodes.description: Individual or system initiating a request (employee, customer, analyst, or automated process). Expresses goals, provides inputs, and reviews outputs.

nodes.name: User Interaction Layer
nodes.description: Entry point to the multi-agent system. Logical grouping of the presentation and orchestration surface through which users engage the system.

nodes.name: Application
nodes.description: Presentation and orchestration surface (web, mobile, chatbot, workflow, or enterprise system). Receives user inputs, forwards them to the agent layer, returns responses, and implements human-in-the-loop controls to review/approve/modify agent outputs before execution or sharing.

human-oversight.description: Human-in-the-loop controls allowing a human to review, approve, or modify agent outputs before they are executed or shared.

config.name: Human-in-the-loop output approval
config.description: Agent outputs must be surfaced to a human for review/approve/modify before execution or external sharing.

nodes.name: Agent Gateway Layer
nodes.description: Controlled entry point into the agent layer. Governs how applications interact with agents and centralizes registration, routing, and guardrails.

nodes.name: Agent Registry
nodes.description: Catalog of available agents, their capabilities, metadata, and configuration. Supports versioning, lifecycle management, and controlled onboarding.

nodes.name: Gateway
nodes.description: Routes requests from the application to the appropriate agent. Validates requests and applies routing rules.

nodes.name: Agent Guardrails and Policies
nodes.description: Enforces a zero-trust model (all inputs untrusted until verified): authentication and authorization, input validation, content filtering, access control, rate limits, and dynamic policy enforcement.

security.description: Zero-trust guardrails for the agent gateway: authn/authz, input validation, content filtering, access control, rate limiting, and dynamic policy enforcement.

config.name: Zero-trust agent gateway enforcement
config.description: All inbound requests to the agent layer are treated as untrusted until authenticated, authorized, validated, content-filtered, and rate-limited under dynamic policy.

nodes.name: Agent Layer
nodes.description: Core reasoning, coordination, and task-execution layer. Interprets validated requests, plans actions, and produces responses.

nodes.name: Agent Collaboration Patterns
nodes.description: Collaboration models used by agents: Supervisor/Worker, Skill-Based Routing, and Agent-as-a-Tool.

nodes.name: Supervisor / Worker
nodes.description: Supervisor agent decomposes complex tasks and dispatches subtasks to worker agents, then aggregates results.

nodes.name: Skill-Based Routing
nodes.description: Routes a request to the agent (or agents) best matched to the required skills/capabilities advertised in the Agent Registry.

nodes.name: Agent as a Tool
nodes.description: Exposes one agent as an invocable tool to another agent, enabling composition through tool-call semantics.

nodes.name: Unified Agent Runtime
nodes.description: Secure, sandboxed execution environment for agents. Contains state management, secure execution, collaboration/handoff, adaptive learning, workspace file system, tools, and memory subsystems.

nodes.name: State Management
nodes.description: Maintains complete task state to support pause, resume, and handoff.

nodes.name: Secure Execution
nodes.description: Intercepts and validates all tool-call requests, and handles execution and credentials securely within the sandbox.

security.description: Runtime sandboxing and tool-call validation: all tool calls are intercepted and validated, and credentials are handled securely within the sandbox.

config.name: Sandboxed secure execution
config.description: All tool-call requests are intercepted and validated; execution and credential handling occur within an isolated sandbox.

nodes.name: Collaboration / Handoff
nodes.description: Enables stateful collaboration between agents via shared memory and state.

nodes.name: Adaptive Learning
nodes.description: Derives learning signals from execution outcomes to refine prompts, configurations, and tool selection.

nodes.name: Workspace File System
nodes.description: Sandboxed, persistent file system for reading and writing files and large artifacts.

nodes.name: Tools Layer
nodes.description: Built-in trusted tools available to the agent runtime.

nodes.name: MCP Client
nodes.description: Secure bridge from the agent runtime to the external MCP Layer.

nodes.name: Shell Tool
nodes.description: Sandboxed shell available to the agent runtime.

nodes.name: I/O Tool
nodes.description: Reads from and writes to the workspace file system.

nodes.name: Web Search Tool
nodes.description: Built-in web search capability.

nodes.name: Short-Term Memory
nodes.description: Immediate context for the reasoning loop.

nodes.name: In-Session Context Manager
nodes.description: Trims and summarizes in-session context to manage context length, cost, and latency.

nodes.name: Long-Term Memory
nodes.description: Durable information retained across sessions, including session summaries and user/task personalization.

nodes.name: Session Summaries
nodes.description: Compressed, durable summaries of prior sessions used to seed and ground future interactions.

nodes.name: User / Task Personalization
nodes.description: Durable user- and task-level personalization signals (preferences, prior context, task affinity) retained across sessions.

nodes.name: Knowledge Layer
nodes.description: Information foundation for grounding and retrieval.

nodes.name: Source Bases
nodes.description: Internal and external repositories (structured, unstructured, graph) providing authoritative grounding information.

nodes.name: Vector DBs
nodes.description: Vector-based retrieval stores supporting semantic search and grounding.

nodes.name: LLM Layer
nodes.description: Controlled interface to approved LLMs. Standardizes inference requests and enforces model-level policies.

nodes.name: Model Registry
nodes.description: Catalog of approved models, metadata, versions, configuration, and usage constraints. Supports lifecycle and onboarding.

nodes.name: LLM Gateway
nodes.description: Routes inference requests to the appropriate model. Validates requests and applies routing rules.

nodes.name: LLM Guardrails and Policies
nodes.description: Zero-trust controls for model interactions applied before and after model execution: per-request authn/authz, input validation (prompt injection), output filtering (redact sensitive / block harmful), access control, rate limits, and cost management.

security.description: Zero-trust guardrails for LLM interactions applied before and after inference.

config.name: Zero-trust LLM guardrails
config.description: Per-request authn/authz, prompt-injection input validation, output filtering (redaction/harm-blocking), access control, rate limiting, and cost management enforced around model execution.

nodes.name: MCP Layer
nodes.description: Controlled interface to approved MCP servers. Standardizes connections and enforces operational and policy constraints.

nodes.name: MCP Server Registry
nodes.description: Catalog of approved MCP servers, metadata, capabilities, configuration, and constraints. Supports lifecycle and onboarding.

nodes.name: MCP Gateway
nodes.description: Routes MCP connection requests to the appropriate server. Validates requests and applies routing rules.

nodes.name: MCP Guardrails and Policies
nodes.description: Zero-trust controls for tool/service interactions via MCP: per-request authn/authz, governance of which agents may use which tools, rate limits, and validation of tool-call parameters.

security.description: Zero-trust guardrails governing tool/service interactions over MCP.

config.name: Zero-trust MCP guardrails
config.description: Per-request authn/authz, agent-to-tool authorization governance, rate limiting, and tool-call parameter validation for all MCP interactions.

nodes.name: Evaluation Layer
nodes.description: Continuous oversight, quality assurance, and adaptive improvement. Operates bidirectionally with the agent layer for real-time intervention and post-execution evaluation.

nodes.name: Feedback Engine
nodes.description: Collects structured and unstructured feedback from users, evaluators, and automated systems, and aggregates the resulting signals.

nodes.name: Human Supervision
nodes.description: Human reviewers inspect, approve, correct, or override agent outputs. Supports human-in-the-loop and human-over-the-loop oversight.

human-oversight.description: Human supervision controls: human reviewers can inspect, approve, correct, or override agent outputs (human-in-the-loop and human-over-the-loop).

config.name: Human supervision and override
config.description: Human reviewers must be able to inspect, approve, correct, or override agent outputs in real time (human-in-the-loop) and after the fact (human-over-the-loop).

nodes.name: Runtime Protection
nodes.description: Monitors agent actions and model outputs during execution; detects unsafe, non-compliant, or anomalous behavior; enforces policy, blocks actions, and triggers escalation.

security.description: Runtime protection controls: continuous monitoring of agent actions and model outputs with policy enforcement, action blocking, and escalation.

config.name: Runtime protection and policy enforcement
config.description: Agent actions and model outputs are monitored during execution to detect unsafe/non-compliant/anomalous behavior, enforce policy, block actions, and trigger escalation.

nodes.name: Observability Layer
nodes.description: End-to-end visibility layer. All layers emit signals into it; it does not send data back.

nodes.name: Logs
nodes.description: Log signal collection within the observability layer.

nodes.name: Traces
nodes.description: Distributed trace collection within the observability layer.

nodes.name: Metrics
nodes.description: Metric collection within the observability layer.

nodes.name: Events
nodes.description: Event collection within the observability layer.

nodes.name: Anomaly Detection
nodes.description: Detects anomalous behavior across collected observability signals.

nodes.name: Correlation Engine
nodes.description: Correlates signals across logs, traces, metrics, and events for end-to-end visibility.

relationships.description: User Interaction Layer is composed of the Application.
Agent Gateway Layer is composed of the Agent Registry, Gateway, and Guardrails and Policies.
Agent Layer is composed of the Collaboration Patterns and the Unified Agent Runtime.
Agent Collaboration Patterns is composed of Supervisor/Worker, Skill-Based Routing, and Agent-as-a-Tool.
Unified Agent Runtime is composed of its state, execution, collaboration, learning, file system, tools, and memory subsystems.
Tools Layer is composed of the built-in trusted tools.
Short-Term Memory is composed of the In-Session Context Manager.
Long-Term Memory is composed of Session Summaries and User/Task Personalization.
Knowledge Layer is composed of the Source Bases and Vector DBs.
LLM Layer is composed of the Model Registry, LLM Gateway, and Guardrails and Policies.
MCP Layer is composed of the MCP Server Registry, MCP Gateway, and Guardrails and Policies.
Evaluation Layer is composed of the Feedback Engine, Human Supervision, and Runtime Protection.
Observability Layer is composed of its signal-collection and analysis components.
User submits goals/inputs to and reviews outputs from the Application.
Application forwards requests to the Agent Gateway and receives responses.
Agent Gateway routes validated requests into the Agent Layer.
Agent Layer issues inference requests to the LLM Layer and receives responses.
Agent Layer connects to the MCP Layer to invoke external tools/services.
Agent Layer retrieves grounding information from the Knowledge Layer.
Evaluation Layer pushes interventions and assessments toward the Agent Layer (forward leg of bidirectional Evaluation <-> Agent).
Agent Layer sends execution context to the Evaluation Layer (return leg of bidirectional Evaluation <-> Agent).
User Interaction Layer emits interaction logs and session events to the Observability Layer (one-directional).
Agent Gateway Layer emits routing, policy, and access signals to the Observability Layer (one-directional).
Agent Layer emits reasoning traces, refinement, and execution metrics to the Observability Layer (one-directional).
LLM Layer emits routing, policy, and access signals to the Observability Layer (one-directional).
MCP Layer emits routing, policy, and access signals to the Observability Layer (one-directional).
Knowledge Layer emits retrieval metrics and search traces to the Observability Layer (one-directional).
Evaluation Layer emits feedback events, supervision outcomes, and runtime protection signals to the Observability Layer (one-directional).

metadata.summary: Modular, governed, extensible multi-agent system for enterprise. Orchestrates agents, models, and tools while enforcing a zero-trust security model ensuring safety, compliance, observability, and human oversight.
